name: DataTable
description: Data tables present sets of records across rows and columns — the primary way operators scan, sort, select, and act on fleet data (vehicles, trips, drivers, alerts). This is the most-used component in the portal, so it carries the most behaviour.
keyRule: When a dataset is large, server-paginated, sortable, or selectable, use the data table — not a basic Table. Reserve the basic table for small, static key/value layouts.

variants.name: With toolbar
variants.whenToUse: Almost always — gives the table a title plus search/filter/export entry points (compose them into toolbarActions from TextField/Button/Menu).

variants.name: Selectable (checkboxes)
variants.whenToUse: When users perform bulk actions (assign, export, delete) on multiple records.

variants.name: Paginated
variants.whenToUse: Any dataset beyond a single screen; desktop tables paginate (rows-per-page + range), they don't infinite-scroll. Works client-side or server-driven — see the Pagination behavior.

variants.name: Dense
variants.whenToUse: High-volume operator screens where maximizing visible rows matters; reduces row height.

variants.name: Sticky header + bounded height
variants.whenToUse: Tall tables inside a fixed layout: set maxHeight to bound the body and stickyHeader so column labels stay visible while scrolling.

variants.name: Loading
variants.whenToUse: While a page of data is being fetched — an indeterminate linear bar under the toolbar; row interactions are disabled until it resolves.

variants.name: Empty state
variants.whenToUse: Zero rows and not loading — a single full-width row with a centered message (customisable via emptyState).

variants.name: Row actions
variants.whenToUse: Secondary per-record actions behind a trailing ⋮ menu (Edit, Duplicate, Delete). Never the record's primary action — that stays a visible control or row click.

anatomy.name: Toolbar
anatomy.description: table title + the toolbarActions slot for table-level controls (search, filter, more). Becomes the contextual toolbar when rows are selected.

anatomy.name: Loading bar
anatomy.description: indeterminate linear ProgressIndicator directly under the toolbar while loading is true.

anatomy.name: Column header
anatomy.description: the label for each column; sortable headers show a direction arrow and an active state. With stickyHeader, header cells pin to the top of the scroll container.

anatomy.name: Sort indicator
anatomy.description: arrow showing ascending/descending on the active sort column.

anatomy.name: Selection column
anatomy.description: optional leading checkboxes; the header checkbox selects/clears all (indeterminate when partial).

anatomy.name: Row
anatomy.description: one record; supports hover, selected, and (optionally) clickable states.

anatomy.name: Cell
anatomy.description: one value; text left-aligned, numbers right-aligned with tabular figures.

anatomy.name: Row actions column
anatomy.description: optional trailing ⋮ icon-button per row opening a Menu of secondary actions (destructive ones in error colour).

anatomy.name: Empty state row
anatomy.description: when there are zero rows and the table isn't loading: one full-width row (real <td colspan>) with a centered message.

anatomy.name: Pagination footer
anatomy.description: rows-per-page, the visible range, and previous/next controls. Sits below the scroll region, never inside it.

anatomy.name: Contextual toolbar
anatomy.description: replaces the title bar while rows are selected, showing the count and bulk actions.

behaviors.name: Sorting
behaviors.description: Clicking a sortable header sorts by that column; clicking again reverses direction. The active column shows a filled label and a direction arrow; only one column sorts at a time (unless multi-sort is explicitly enabled).

behaviors.name: Selection
behaviors.description: Row checkboxes select individual records; the header checkbox selects all on the page and shows an indeterminate state when the selection is partial. While anything is selected, the toolbar switches to the contextual toolbar — showing the count and bulk actions (e.g. Export, Delete) — and selected rows take the selected background.

behaviors.name: Pagination
behaviors.description: The footer shows rows-per-page, the current range, and prev/next. Server-driven pagination is supported today with the same props: pass the current page slice as rows, the full count as totalRows, and fetch the next slice in onPageChange instead of slicing a client-side array. Prefer server-driven past a few thousand rows, or whenever the full dataset isn't practical to hold client-side.

behaviors.name: Scrolling
behaviors.description: Horizontal scroll is always available when columns exceed the container. Setting maxHeight bounds the body vertically; with stickyHeader the header row stays pinned (surface fill + divider boundary line) while rows scroll beneath. Toolbar and pagination stay outside the scroll region.

behaviors.name: Loading
behaviors.description: loading renders an indeterminate linear ProgressIndicator under the toolbar, sets aria-busy, and disables sort, selection, row click, row actions, and paging until data arrives — matching the fleet-web convention of blocking interaction during fetch. For content loading into a known layout, a future Skeleton component is the eventual alternative.

behaviors.name: Empty state
behaviors.description: Zero rows (and not loading) renders a single centered 'No data' row spanning all columns; pass emptyState to add a tailored message or action. This is a minimal placeholder — a dedicated Empty state component is tracked separately and will supersede the default content.

behaviors.name: Row actions
behaviors.description: rowActions renders a trailing ⋮ button per row (aria-haspopup, Enter/Space to open) showing a Menu of the returned items; Escape or an outside click closes it, and destructive items render in the error colour. Row click handlers don't fire from inside the actions cell.

deferred.name: Column resizing / reordering / pinning / visibility toggle
deferred.why: MUI-X DataGrid extensions in fleet-web, not MD2-native; substantial to build without a library. Future work — use the real DataGrid pattern if a screen needs them today.

deferred.name: Export (CSV/Excel/PDF)
deferred.why: Significant scope. Compose an export control into toolbarActions instead — DataTable intentionally doesn't own export.

deferred.name: Row expansion / detail panel
deferred.why: Not part of the MD2 data-table contract; hierarchical 'table tree' rows are tracked as their own future component.

deferred.name: Inline cell editing
deferred.why: Depends on the future dedicated Inline edit component; the previously documented 'Inline-editable' variant had no implementation and has been removed until then.

deferred.name: Multi-column sort
deferred.why: Stays opt-out per the existing don't rule; no change planned.

states.treatment: Arrow fades in at ~50%; cursor pointer.
Label at full on-surface; arrow fully visible.
Header cells pinned top with surface fill and a 1px divider boundary line so rows don't show through.
Filled with a dash when selection is partial.
Indeterminate linear bar under the toolbar; aria-busy set; sort/selection/row/paging interactions disabled.
Single full-width row, centered secondary-text message (default 'No data').

specs.value: Roboto 14px / 500, color on-surface-medium (full on-surface when sorted)
Roboto 14px / 400, color on-surface; numbers right-aligned, tabular figures
surface fill + 1px divider-colour line while pinned

props.name: title
props.description: Table title shown in the toolbar (replaced by the contextual toolbar during selection).

props.name: columns
props.description: Column definitions: key, header label, numeric alignment, sortability, custom render.

props.name: rows
props.description: Records to display; each row must have a unique id. For server-driven pagination this is just the current page's slice.

props.name: selectable
props.description: Enables the leading selection checkbox column and the contextual toolbar.

props.name: selectedIds
props.description: Currently selected row ids.

props.name: onSelectionChange
props.description: Called when row/select-all checkboxes change selection.

props.name: bulkActions
props.description: Bulk actions shown in the contextual toolbar while rows are selected (e.g. Export, Delete).

props.name: sortColumn
props.description: Active sort column key.

props.name: sortDirection
props.description: Direction of the active sort.

props.name: onSortChange
props.description: Called when a sortable header is clicked.

props.name: dense
props.description: Reduces row/header height for high-volume operator screens.

props.name: page
props.description: Current zero-based page index; omit to render an unpaginated table. Client-side or server-driven — the component doesn't care where the page slice came from.

props.name: rowsPerPage
props.description: Rows shown per page.

props.name: totalRows
props.description: Total record count for the visible-range and next/prev disabled state. For server-driven pagination this is the server's full count, not rows.length.

props.name: onPageChange
props.description: Called when prev/next pagination controls are used. For server-driven pagination, fetch the new page's slice here and pass it back as rows — that's the whole server-pagination contract; prefer it past a few thousand rows or when the dataset can't be held client-side.

props.name: onRowClick
props.description: Handler for clickable rows. Not fired from clicks inside the row-actions cell.

props.name: stickyHeader
props.description: Keeps the header row pinned to the top of the scroll container while the body scrolls — pair with maxHeight (closes the promise in templates/table-page.md, 'header sticky on scroll').

props.name: maxHeight
props.description: Bounds the table body; vertical scroll appears beyond it. Horizontal scroll on overflow is always available regardless. Toolbar and pagination footer stay outside the scroll region.

props.name: loading
props.description: Shows an indeterminate linear ProgressIndicator under the toolbar, sets aria-busy, and disables sort/selection/row-click/row-actions/paging while data loads.

props.name: emptyState
props.description: Custom zero-rows content (message and/or action) rendered in a single full-width row; defaults to 'No data'. Minimal placeholder until the dedicated Empty state component exists.

props.name: rowActions
props.type: (row: Row) => { label: string; onClick: (row: Row) => void; destructive?: boolean }[]
props.description: Per-row overflow actions: renders a trailing ⋮ icon-button that opens a Menu with these items (destructive ones in error colour). The canonical version of the per-row ⋮ pattern from templates/table-page.md — don't hand-roll it per module.

props.name: toolbarActions
props.description: Caller-composed controls rendered beside the title in the default toolbar (search TextField, filter Button, export trigger…). Intentionally generic — DataTable does not implement search, filtering, or export itself.

doThis: Use the data table (not a basic table) for any sortable, selectable, or large dataset.
Right-align numeric columns and use tabular figures so digits line up.
Switch to the contextual toolbar when rows are selected, showing the count + bulk actions.
Map status to the consistent colour chips (Active/Idle/Offline).
Paginate; fetch per page (server-driven) for large data — pass the page slice as rows and the full count as totalRows.
Pair maxHeight with stickyHeader on tall tables so column labels stay visible while scanning.
Use rowActions for per-row secondary actions instead of hand-rolling a ⋮ menu in every module.
dontDoThis: Don't put the primary action inside a row's overflow menu if it's used constantly.
Don't sort more than one column at once unless multi-sort is explicitly needed.
Don't encode status by colour alone — keep the text label on the chip.
Don't infinite-scroll a desktop data table; use pagination.
Don't hard-code colours/sizes — use the theme tokens and these specs.
Don't build export/filter/search into DataTable — compose them into toolbarActions from existing components.
Don't leave a fetching table empty — use loading, and let the empty state appear only when the fetch genuinely returned zero rows.
accessibility: Use real <table> semantics with <th> headers; sortable headers expose aria-sort.
The select-all checkbox announces its indeterminate state.
Selection changes and the contextual toolbar are announced to assistive tech.
Rows and controls are keyboard navigable; visible focus is preserved.
Status is never colour-only — the chip carries a text label.
loading sets aria-busy on the table and renders a role=progressbar with an accessible label; disabled controls leave the tab order.
The empty state keeps valid table semantics (a real <tr>/<td colspan>, not a replacement <div>).
The row-actions trigger is a real <button> (Enter/Space) with aria-haspopup and aria-expanded; the menu closes on Escape or outside click.

related.name: Pagination
related.why: Always paired — never an unpaginated long table

related.name: ProgressIndicator
related.why: Renders the loading state's linear bar

related.name: List
related.why: Single-column content doesn't need a table

related.name: Card
related.why: Summarise aggregates in cards above the table
